name: Construction System
summary: Steward, Settlers, Settlements, Territories, HealthOS Forge MCP doctrine, repository-local construction roots, and agent infrastructure.
invariants: Steward, Settlers, Settlements, Territories, and HealthOS Forge MCP are outside the HealthOS clinical/runtime hierarchy.
Official docs remain canonical; derived memory and construction records remain subordinate.
Construction tooling has no merge, clinical, Core-law, regulatory, or production-readiness authority.
HealthOS Forge MCP and healthos-forge-mcp naming supersede deprecated repository-maintenance MCP naming.
allowedWork: Maintain construction-system docs, schemas, territory records, Settler profiles, and Settlement records.
Maintain deterministic healthos-steward behavior only where implemented and validated.
Maintain HealthOS Forge MCP repository-maintenance seams without expanding them into clinical/runtime authority.
Update trackers and handoff notes for construction tasks.
forbiddenWork: Make construction tooling clinical runtime or Core law.
Give Steward or Settlers merge, clinical, regulatory, or autonomous execution authority.
Use deprecated repository-maintenance MCP naming as canonical.
Claim unimplemented construction capabilities, Settler execution, runtime MCP behavior, or Managed Agent live API behavior before source and smoke evidence confirms them.
validationCommands: for f in HealthOS/Constructor/Settler/territories/*.json; do python3 -m json.tool "$f" >/dev/null; done
knownGaps: Settler execution runtime is not implemented.
No autonomous construction executor or merge authority exists.
No HealthOS clinical/runtime MCP server is implemented.
Managed Agent workflow code exists as an implemented seam, but live API execution depends on external credentials and a publicly reachable FORGE_MCP_URL.
notes: This ST-011 registry is construction metadata only.

`HealthOS/Constructor/ts/agent-infra/mcp-local` was removed in ST-019 (2026-05-05) — it was an unused stub with clinical tool names; `HealthOS/Constructor/ts/agent-infra/healthos-forge-mcp` (ST-018) is the implemented repository-maintenance MCP surface.
